intro: Alysicarpus campylocaulis is a type of legume plant, commonly known as a shrub or a climber, native to various parts of the world including Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Alysicarpus campylocaulis include flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and fever.
